images.caption: Figure 1:Overview ofUDSfor a single forget set example.(A)Stage 1 patches hidden states fromMretM_{\text{ret}}intoMfullM_{\text{full}}at each layer to measure how deeply the forget set knowledge is encoded.(B)Stage 2 repeats this withMunlM_{\text{unl}}as source to quantify how much encoded knowledge remains recoverable.(C)Stage 2 degradation is compared against Stage 1 at each layer to compute erasure ratios, which are weighted and aggregated into a single 0–1 score.

Figure 2:Quantization test for Truth Ratio and ROUGE.
Truth Ratio dots lie along the diagonal, indicating stable scores; ROUGE dots in the blue box fall below the diagonal.
One-directional formulas do not penalize this decline, but our symmetric formula (Eq.8) does.

Figure 3:Faithfulness evaluation of four white-box metrics via P/N pool separation.
CKA measures geometry and Fisher measures gradient sensitivity, neither of which directly reflects knowledge content, yielding poor separation.
Logit Lens andUDSdistinguish the pools well;UDSfurther benefits from causal intervention.

Figure 4:Mean S1 patching delta per layer for four patching locations. Patching the full layer outputhlh_{l}(used byUDS) captures the dominant share of the knowledge, with the gap widening in later layers. Shaded regions denote±\pm1 standard deviation.

Figure 5:Per-exampleUDSvs. entity token count (lr=2e-5, epoch 10; UNDIAL uses lr=1e-4). RMU variants differ by the target layerllat which the steering loss is applied (L5, L10, L15). All methods show|ρ|<0.24|\rho|<0.24with mixed sign, indicating no consistent directional bias.

Figure 6:Quantization robustness (QQ) for all 20 metrics after utility and faithfulness filtering.
Each subplot plots the metric value before (xx) vs. after (yy) NF4 4-bit quantization, with the number of models showing recovery (rec) or destruction (des).nnis the number of models that passed both filters for that metric.
The background gradient indicates deviation from they=xy=xreference: white = stable, red = unstable.

Figure 7:Quantization robustness (QQ) for all 20 metrics after utility filtering only.
Each subplot plots the metric value before (xx) vs. after (yy) NF4 4-bit quantization, with the number of models showing recovery (rec) or destruction (des).nnis the number of models that passed the utility filter for that metric.
The background gradient indicates deviation from they=xy=xreference: white = stable, red = unstable.
Metrics such as Extraction Strength and ROUGE show score drops across many models after quantization.

Figure 8:Relearning robustness (RR) for all 20 metrics after utility and faithfulness filtering.
Each subplot plots the metric value before (xx) vs. after (yy) one epoch of relearning, with the number of models showing over-recovery (over) or under-recovery (under).nnis the number of models that passed both filters for that metric.
The dashed line showsy=x+Δrety=x+\Delta_{\text{ret}}(expected behavior given the retain model’s shift); the dotted line showsy=xy=x.
The background gradient indicates deviation from the expected line: white = stable, red = unstable.
The red star marks the retain model.
